game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Geheimnis Blackthorn Castle 2:** Players navigate a 14th-century castle environment by tapping on interactive hotspots to move between static screens, examine objects, or pick up items into an inventory. To solve the dozens of integrated puzzles, players must tap and drag inventory items onto environmental elements or combine them within the inventory, such as using a key on a locked chest or combining two gears. Progression is driven by solving these puzzles, which unlocks new areas of the castle, reveals hidden paths, or advances the narrative, with a dynamic map accessible via a UI button to track visited locations. A camera tool, activated by a tap, allows players to photograph up to 10 clues or symbols for later reference, and a hint system, also accessed via a UI button, provides tiered guidance after a 30-second cooldown per hint. The game features an auto-save function every 5 minutes or upon entering a new major area, ensuring continuous progress towards the ultimate goal of completing the adventure.
game_info.description: Explore the mysterious Blackthorn Castle, a 14th-century fortress filled with secrets and puzzles. Navigate through interconnected rooms by clicking on doorways and interactive objects. Collect items into your inventory and use them to solve puzzles that unlock new areas. Your goal is to explore the entire castle, solve all puzzles, and uncover the castle's deepest secret hidden in the throne room. Track your progress with the map, take photos of clues with your camera, and use hints when stuck.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate cursor for selecting hotspots and UI buttons
Space: Select/activate highlighted hotspot or button
Z: Open/close inventory panel
Shift: Use camera to photograph current room (up to 10 photos)
Enter: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart and return to title screen

TEST_1.test_description: Test basic navigation and interaction mechanics. Verify that the player can move between rooms, interact with hotspots, pick up items, and access UI features like inventory and map. This validates core gameplay loops.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through several rooms in sequence, interact with various hotspots to pick up items, open the inventory to verify items are stored, and access the map to confirm room tracking. Test both successful and unsuccessful interactions.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves between at least 3 rooms, collects at least 2 items into inventory, opens and closes inventory panel, and accesses the map. All interactions respond correctly without errors. Test passes if all basic mechanics function as expected.

TEST_2.test_description: Test winning the game by solving all puzzles and reaching the final objective. This validates that the game can be completed through proper puzzle-solving sequences and that the win condition triggers correctly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow an optimal path through the castle: collect key items in correct order, use them to solve puzzles that unlock new areas, and progress methodically toward the throne room. Solve puzzles efficiently using knowledge of correct item combinations and sequences.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player successfully solves all required puzzles, unlocks all necessary areas, reaches the throne room, and triggers the GAME_OVER_WIN condition. The game displays the victory screen with completion message and final statistics.
